channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UN Prosecutor Concludes Talks on Genocide Justice in Rwanda
item.description: The chief prosecutor of the United Nations International Residual Mechanism for Criminal Tribunals, Serge Brammertz, has finished his official visit to Rwanda. During this visit, he held high-level me...

item.title: Russia Offers Support Amid Ebola Outbreak in Uganda and DRC
item.description: Russia has expressed deep concern over the recent Ebola outbreak affecting Uganda and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). Speaking on Thursday, Maria Zakharova, spokesperson for the Russian Fo...

item.title: Tensions in Durban as Police Intervene in Clashes Between Foreign Nationals and Anti-Immigrant Groups
item.description: On Thursday, outside the Diakonia Centre in Durban, tensions flared between foreign nationals seeking refuge and members of anti-immigrant groups, including March and March. The dispute reportedly beg...

item.title: Challenges in Managing the Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak in the DRC
item.description: A rare strain of Ebola, known as Bundibugyo, is spreading in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), a region affected by conflict and displacement. Since April, over 130 deaths and more than ...

item.title: Supreme Court of Canada to rule on B.C. Indigenous mineral claims dispute
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada is set to determine whether it will hear British Columbia’s appeal regarding a significant legal dispute over mineral claims and Indigenous rights. In December, the B.C. Co...

item.title: Evelyn Hofer’s Timeless Fashion Photography from the 1950s and 1960s
item.description: Evelyn Hofer was a pioneering fashion photographer whose work during the 1950s and 1960s distinguished itself through a serene, meticulous approach. Unlike many of her contemporaries, Hofer avoided th...

item.title: Nvidia Earnings to Signal Future Growth in AI Semiconductor Market
item.description: Nvidia Corp., a leading AI semiconductor company, is set to release its earnings report, which could confirm whether the ongoing rally in chip stocks has room to continue or signal caution for investo...

item.title: Kerala Congress leaders raise concerns over CM Satheesan using full name during oath
item.description: Just four days after assuming office as Kerala's Chief Minister, Congress leader VD Satheesan has found himself at the centre of a debate not about governance but about identity. Satheesan used his fu...

item.title: Vizio Faces Trial Over Linux-Based Smart TV Source Code Access
item.description: The Software Freedom Conservancy (SFC), a nonprofit advocating for open source software, is taking Vizio to court over its refusal to release the full source code for its Linux-based smart TV operatin...

item.title: Authorities arrest 49 foreign nationals for immigration violations in Davao, Pasay, and Pampanga
item.description: Authorities from the Philippine Bureau of Immigration (BI) apprehended a total of 49 foreign nationals in Davao de Oro, Pasay City, and Pampanga for allegedly violating immigration laws and engaging i...

item.title: PIA Successfully Completes Pre-Haj Flights Transporting Over 53,000 Pilgrims
item.description: Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) has successfully concluded its pre-Haj flight operations for the year, transporting over 53,000 pilgrims to Saudi Arabia via a total of 210 flights, a PIA spokesp...

item.title: McDonald’s Philippines switches to renewable energy with Shell Energy partnership
item.description: McDonald’s Philippines is taking steps towards sustainability by partnering with Shell Energy to implement renewable energy across its operations. This initiative is part of the fast-food chain's comm...

item.title: Understanding Nonprofit Fraud: Investigations, Oversight, and Donor Risks
item.description: Recent high-profile prosecutions of nonprofit organizations have brought nonprofit fraud into the spotlight. In Minnesota, federal authorities prosecuted a major COVID-19 pandemic-related fraud case, ...

item.title: French Open 2026 draw reveals first-round matchups for top seeds
item.description: The draw for the 2026 French Open has been completed, setting the stage for an exciting tournament at Roland Garros. Italy's Jannik Sinner has emerged as the top seed in the men’s draw following the w...

item.title: Former Epstein Assistant Testifies About Ongoing Abuse During His Incarceration
item.description: Sarah Kellen, a former assistant to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, is scheduled to testify before the House Oversight Committee, detailing the abuse she suffered and the control Epstein exert...

item.title: Kwankwaso Denies Working For Tinubu, Reaffirms Loyalty To NDC
item.description: Former Kano State governor, Rabiu Kwankwaso don clear matter say e no dey work for President Bola Tinubu for the 2027 presidential election. During interview with Global TV on Wednesday night, Kwankwa...

item.title: Apple TV’s 'Margo’s Got Money Troubles' Explores the Realities of OnlyFans Work
item.description: Apple TV+’s adaptation of Rufi Thorpe’s novel, 'Margo’s Got Money Troubles,' offers a nuanced portrayal of sex work, specifically through the lens of OnlyFans. The series follows Margo Millet, a 20-ye...

item.title: Islamabad Traffic Police implements special plan for Eid ul Adha cattle markets
item.description: Islamabad Traffic Police (ITP) has finalised a detailed traffic management plan for the upcoming Eid ul Adha, specifically targeting smooth movement around the city's cattle markets. Chief Traffic Off...

item.title: University of Dar es Salaam introduces digital platform to guide postgraduate researchers
item.description: The University of Dar es Salaam has unveiled a new digital platform called SKN Research Compass, designed to help postgraduate students overcome common research challenges. Many Tanzanian students fac...

item.title: Kenya court exempts adolescents from certain sexual offence charges for consensual relationships
item.description: On Wednesday, a Kenyan court ruled that some sections of the Sexual Offences Act cannot be applied to minors who engage in consensual sex. The judgement came after rights groups, including the Centre ...
